topStories.description: The SC also clarified that neither the High Court order which granted Khera relief not the apex court order which stayed the relief shall come in the way of a court in Assam which would hear and decide the matter

topStories.title: BBC in Iran: 'Tehran does not think it has lost this war'
topStories.description: Lyse Doucet says Iranians want a solution to the long-running animosity with the US, but leaders is not willing to make a deal on Washington's terms.

topStories.title: US-Iran war impact: West Asia may take up to 2 years to restore lost energy production, says IEA
topStories.description: Energy production in West Asia may take two years to recover post-conflict, varying by country, according to the IEA. The US and UK are discussing maritime security in the Strait of Hormuz, while a ceasefire with Iran may expire on April 22.

topStories.title: PM Modi's appeal for vote on women's quota amid questions over delimitation link
topStories.description: Congress-led Opposition has said a biased delimitation law is being pushed through while using the women quota as smokescreen.

topStories.title: Quote of the Day by Maya Angelou: 'I don't trust people who don't love themselves and tell me ‘I love you’…'
topStories.description: Maya Angelou was a prolific American writer and civil rights activist, known for her seven autobiographies and powerful poetry.

topStories.title: ‘Hungry all the time’: Families sound alarm as US troops are served ‘dry patty, tasteless food’ on their way to Mideast
topStories.description: A picture of a dinner on the USS Abraham Lincoln, shared by a service member with his family - consisted of a small handful of boiled carrots, a dry meat patty and a gray slab of processed meat

topStories.title: 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire takes effect after intense fight — all you need to know about truce
topStories.description: A 10-day truce holds in Lebanon, pausing Israel-Hezbollah fighting and easing tensions for a potential Iran-U.S.-Israel deal. Residents celebrate, but uncertainty remains over Israel's military actions and Hezbollah's recognition of the deal.

topStories.title: Harivansh re-elected unopposed as Rajya Sabha deputy chairman; oppo walks out
topStories.description: Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge said the government should have first filled the Lok Sabha deputy speaker’s post, which has been vacant since 2019

topStories.title: Lebanon Cease-Fire Leaves Netanyahu in an Uncomfortable Spot
topStories.description: Most Israelis wanted the fight against Hezbollah to continue. But Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s critics say he is showing that he cannot, or will not, stand up to President Trump.

topStories.title: What to Know About the Israel-Lebanon Cease-Fire, and R.F.K. Jr.’s Shifting Tone on Vaccines
topStories.description: Plus, the Friday news quiz.

topStories.title: House Votes to Extend Expiring FISA Surveillance Law for 10 Days
topStories.description: The Senate would need to also approve the stopgap measure that passed the House early Friday. Libertarian-leaning House Republicans had balked at a long-term extension.

topStories.title: She Made Sure Her Baby Was Born an American. Then Federal Agents Separated Them.
topStories.description: Diana Acosta Verde, who came into the United States illegally when she was six months pregnant, had to leave her baby at a hospital while she returned to a detention center.

dailyNews.description: The SC also clarified that neither the High Court order which granted Khera relief not the apex court order which stayed the relief shall come in the way of a court in Assam which would hear and decide the matter

dailyNews.description: Most Israelis wanted the fight against Hezbollah to continue. But Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s critics say he is showing that he cannot, or will not, stand up to President Trump.

dailyNews.title: What to Know About the Israel-Lebanon Cease-Fire, and R.F.K. Jr.’s Shifting Tone on Vaccines
dailyNews.description: Plus, the Friday news quiz.

dailyNews.title: House Votes to Extend Expiring FISA Surveillance Law for 10 Days
dailyNews.description: The Senate would need to also approve the stopgap measure that passed the House early Friday. Libertarian-leaning House Republicans had balked at a long-term extension.

dailyNews.title: She Made Sure Her Baby Was Born an American. Then Federal Agents Separated Them.
dailyNews.description: Diana Acosta Verde, who came into the United States illegally when she was six months pregnant, had to leave her baby at a hospital while she returned to a detention center.

dailyNews.title: ICE Has Detained Marie-Thérèse Ross-Mahé, an 85-Year-Old Widow
dailyNews.description: After Marie-Thérèse Ross-Mahé’s husband died, an inheritance battle exploded. Her stepson then used his influence to have her arrested, an Alabama probate judge said.

dailyNews.title: Acting ICE Director Says He Plans to Resign in May
dailyNews.description: Todd Lyons said he would leave to spend more time with his family. He has spoken about a surge in threats against ICE officers, saying that he knew the reality firsthand.

dailyNews.title: Texas Restaurant Owners Call for Work Permits as Immigration Crackdown Strains Industry
dailyNews.description: Restaurant operators say labor shortages, rising costs and worker fear have prompted an unusual alliance of industry and political leaders in Texas to call for legal pathways to hire immigrants.

dailyNews.title: We Need Lena Dunham Now More Than Ever
dailyNews.description: The era of “Girls” is long gone. So why are we still so fascinated by its creator?

dailyNews.title: Lena Dunham Takes to Her Bed to Promote Her Memoir, “Famesick.”
dailyNews.description: Forget demure conversations in spindly chairs. To promote “Famesick,” a new memoir, she’s taken to her bed and invited friends to jump in. Onstage.

dailyNews.title: In ‘Famesick,’ Lena Dunham Diagnoses Celebrity, Illness and Herself
dailyNews.description: This unusually unfiltered memoir takes us to the hospital, to therapy and to the sometimes hostile set of “Girls.”

dailyNews.title: The 27-Year-Old Diplomat Waging Trump’s Cultural War With Europe
dailyNews.description: Five years out of college, Samuel Samson has driven the Trump administration’s push to upend America’s postwar relationship with Europe.

dailyNews.title: Analilia Mejia, a Progressive Democrat, Wins Mikie Sherrill’s House Seat
dailyNews.description: Ms. Mejia, who helped run Sen. Bernie Sanders’s presidential campaign, beat her Republican opponent, Joe Hathaway, to win a seat Ms. Sherrill vacated after she was elected governor of New Jersey.

dailyNews.title: Trump Tries to Stop Drug Smuggling From South America. Drug Smugglers Invent New Tactics.
dailyNews.description: Authorities are using drones, troops and A.I. to crack down on cocaine smuggling. But gangs are finding new ways to move record amounts of drugs across the world.

dailyNews.title: Trump to Pick Ousted FEMA Head to Lead Agency Again
dailyNews.description: Cameron Hamilton is a former Navy SEAL who ran unsuccessfully for Congress.

dailyNews.title: The Lurid D4vd Case and the Fervid Hunt for Details
dailyNews.description: A teenage girl went missing. The police found her remains in a musician’s car. Then the Los Angeles media machine got to work.

dailyNews.title: D4vd Arrested in Connection With Death of Celeste Rivas Hernandez
dailyNews.description: The singer was detained in connection with the death of Celeste Rivas Hernandez, who died before her 15th birthday. D4vd’s lawyers said they would vigorously defend his innocence.

topStories.title: Lebanon Cease-Fire Leaves Netanyahu in an Uncomfortable Spot
topStories.description: Most Israelis wanted the fight against Hezbollah to continue. But Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s critics say he is showing that he cannot, or will not, stand up to President Trump.

topStories.title: The 27-Year-Old Diplomat Waging Trump’s Cultural War With Europe
topStories.description: Five years out of college, Samuel Samson has driven the Trump administration’s push to upend America’s postwar relationship with Europe.

topStories.description: Authorities are using drones, troops and A.I. to crack down on cocaine smuggling. But gangs are finding new ways to move record amounts of drugs across the world.

topStories.title: Trump to Pick Ousted FEMA Head to Lead Agency Again
topStories.description: Cameron Hamilton is a former Navy SEAL who ran unsuccessfully for Congress.

topStories.title: GREGG JARRETT: Long-hidden documents reveal first Trump impeachment was a total fraud
topStories.description: Newly declassified documents show the first Trump impeachment was orchestrated fraud, DNI Tulsi Gabbard says, sending criminal referrals to the DOJ.

topStories.title: Greenland talks on ‘good trajectory,’ White House says amid Trump takeover push
topStories.description: U.S. officials say negotiations with Denmark and Greenland are moving in a positive direction, even as tensions remain over Trump’s push for greater U.S. control of the strategic Arctic island.

topStories.title: House punts Trump spy powers extension after conservatives block deal, forcing end-of-month showdown
topStories.description: The FISA Section 702 surveillance program hit a temporary roadblock after House conservatives rejected a long-term extension early Friday morning.

topStories.title: Trump admin announces expansion of visa restriction policy in Western Hemisphere
topStories.description: The State Department said it imposed visa restrictions on 26 individuals under the expanded policy targeting those acting for U.S. adversaries.

topStories.title: Miami Catholic Charities loses $11M contract providing care for migrant children under Trump administration
topStories.description: The Trump administration canceled an $11 million Catholic Charities contract in Miami, citing a drop in illegal immigration and fewer unaccompanied minors.

topStories.title: Controversial surveillance program extended by House but only until April 30
topStories.description: The House OK'd a short-term renewal of a controversial surveillance program used by U.S. spy agencies, after Republicans revolted and refused President Trump's push for a longer extension.

topStories.title: Where Trump's goals in Iran stand at this point
topStories.description: President Trump has repeatedly claimed that the war with Iran is "almost over." He has also stated multiple times that the U.S. is close to accomplishing "all of its goals" in Iran. CBS News' Robert Costa reports and Michael Doran, director of the Center for Peace and Security in the Middle East at the Hudson Institute, have more.

topStories.title: Trump continues feud with Pope Leo over war with Iran
topStories.description: Earlier this week, President Trump turned his criticism toward an unlikely subject- Pope Leo. Michael O'Loughlin, the executive editor of the National Catholic Reporter, joins "The Daily Report."

dailyNews.description: Earlier this week, President Trump turned his criticism toward an unlikely subject- Pope Leo. Michael O'Loughlin, the executive editor of the National Catholic Reporter, joins "The Daily Report."

dailyNews.title: Trump nominates Dr. Erica Schwartz for new CDC director
dailyNews.description: President Trump nominated a new director for the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention on Thursday. Dr. Erica Schwartz is a former deputy surgeon general and retired Coast Guard rear admiral.

dailyNews.title: Oil falls as Trump reiterates Iran war ‘should’ end soon and Israel-Lebanon truce lifts hopes
dailyNews.description: Oil prices fell after Donald Trump said Israel and Lebanon had agreed to a 10-day ceasefire following talks in Washington.

dailyNews.title: Trump says gas prices 'not very high' as most U.S. voters blame him for price spike
dailyNews.description: Trump touted the rising stock market as he brushed off concerns that Americans are laboring under higher gas prices as a result of the Iran war.

dailyNews.title: Trump nominates Erica Schwartz as CDC director amid turmoil around leadership, vaccine policy
dailyNews.description: The CDC has been going through turmoil and several leadership shakeups under HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.

dailyNews.title: Vance's Iran and Orbán setbacks raise questions about his standing with Trump
dailyNews.description: Recent staffing moves among JD Vance's aides have fueled new speculation that the VP and his allies are preparing for a 2028 presidential bid.

dailyNews.title: Spain touts energy resilience to Iran war as Trump tensions cast shadow over trade
dailyNews.description: The southern European country has ramped up its investment in solar and wind technologies in recent years.

dailyNews.title: Trump announces ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon
dailyNews.description: The ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon could be a major step toward a peace deal for Iran and the US. President Trump says US talks with Iran could restart this weekend, and he’s confident they are close to a deal. NBC News’ Richard Engel has the story.

dailyNews.title: Netanyahu says Israel has not agreed to withdraw its forces from southern Lebanon
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump announced that the leaders of Israel and Lebanon had reached a ceasefire deal as fighting raged between Israel and the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah.

dailyNews.title: House effort to end Trump's war with Iran fails by one vote
dailyNews.description: The Republican-controlled House rejected a resolution ordering President Donald Trump to end the war in Iran.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says Colombia Will Accept Deportees, Ending Tariff Standoff
dailyNews.description: The president has made immigration an early priority to start second term, with ICE making more than 900 arrests Sunday.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says He Wants to 'Clean Out' Gaza, Send Refugees to Egypt and Jordan
dailyNews.description: The president, calling the heavily bombed enclave “a demolition site,” proposed relocating Palestinians temporarily or long-term, a move rejected by Arab countries since the war began.

dailyNews.title: Beijing Signals Readiness to Talk to Trump's Team, Even Old Foes
dailyNews.description: The Chinese foreign minister spoke by phone with Marco Rubio, the new secretary of state who has been a critic of Beijing.
